basics.name: Marcelo Prates, PhD
basics.label: ML Engineer
basics.summary: Senior ML engineer with 7+ years building forecasting, computer vision, and LLM systems. Hands-on with PyTorch model training, Bayesian optimization, uncertainty-aware evaluation, and MLflow-backed experimentation for production deployments.

work.name: HopHR
highlights: Led Moon's production candidate-search platform, unifying retrieval, scoring, reranking, and explainable output so ambiguous hiring requests could become auditable shortlist decisions.
Implemented backend APIs, async workflows, and runtime infrastructure on Supabase/PostgreSQL with Redis-backed caching and queues to support reliable, high-throughput hiring operations.
Improved release safety through CI/CD hardening, test-driven refactors, and stronger observability across search, streaming, and agent-runtime components.
Designed and operated a PydanticAI-centric multi-agent runtime: specialist profiles, delegated subtasks, memory-aware shortlist re-evaluation, and feedback-triggered workflow adaptation.
Strengthened runtime observability with request correlation, trace propagation, safe logging/redaction, and metrics/tracing patterns suited for debugging long-running agent workflows.
Stack: TypeScript, Next.js 15, React, Tailwind, Clerk, Supabase, PostgreSQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, OpenAI APIs, PydanticAI, Vercel AI SDK.
Built a trainable model layer for search with user-scoped persistence in PostgreSQL/Supabase, quality tiers, and semantic model discovery.
Developed model-to-filter translation that turns learned SHAP/feature-importance signals into executable Elasticsearch filter clauses.
Added uncertainty-aware prediction paths (Gaussian Process and conformal intervals) for calibrated candidate scoring.
Established an analytics loop: criterion-level rerank scores → train models → translate insights into filters → rerun and calibrate searches.
Partnered with recruiters and hiring managers to convert ambiguous role requirements into testable search/ranking hypotheses.

work.name: Dataside
highlights: Promoted to Lead Data Scientist; mentored team members and owned client-facing discovery, solution design, and technical delivery across multiple accounts.
Standardized evaluation, documentation, deployment, and stakeholder-alignment routines so ambiguous business problems became measurable ML roadmaps, data requirements, and KPIs.
Hardened the retrieval layer with safe vector-store initialization, advisory-lock-based schema setup, embedding-dimension safeguards, and non-destructive defaults for live indexed data.

Designed autonomous fallback paths and regression QA workflows — CLI probes and Playwright-backed browser validation — so assistants remained usable when orchestration dependencies or credentials were unavailable.
Delivered full-stack agentic applications with FastAPI backend and React frontend, deployed to Azure Container Apps with Grafana observability and Docker Compose orchestration.
Built MLflow-tracked forecasting pipelines with Bayesian hyperparameter optimization, time-series cross-validation, and model comparison across XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, and sklearn regressors.
Added uncertainty-aware outputs with MAPIE/conformal intervals plus business-aware evaluation combining MAE/sMAPE, under/overestimation tradeoffs, and calibrated reliability thresholds.
Designed promotion-grade optimization and validation loops that improved forecast quality against baseline while preserving business credibility and deployability.

Built MLflow-tracked hierarchical demand-forecasting systems on Databricks with time-series feature engineering, business-aware loss functions, and conformal uncertainty calibration to reduce error while controlling underforecast risk.

Benchmarked and tuned diverse regression families, including linear and regularized models, tree ensembles, kernel methods, neural nets, XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost, using time-series cross-validation and Bayesian hyperparameter optimization.

Developed imbalance-aware hierarchical classifiers for large product taxonomies using TF-IDF and gradient boosting, plus entropy-based abstention thresholds and human-review loops to balance accuracy, coverage, and operational workload.

Delivered unsupervised and decision-science solutions including clustering/segmentation, anomaly detection with SHAP-based explanations, experimentation/A-B testing, and hybrid NLP pipelines combining TF-IDF, BM25, embeddings, UMAP, HDBSCAN, and vector search.

Architected production ML pipelines with PySpark, SQL, Azure, Databricks Jobs, Azure DevOps CI/CD, MLflow model registry, containerized train/predict workflows, and scheduled retraining or model-renewal loops.

work.name: SingularityAI (Part-Time)
highlights: Led end-to-end development of AI voice assistant with real-time transcription, TTS generation, and persona-aware RAG responses.
Built YouTube→ChromaDB pipeline with AssemblyAI transcription, speaker diarization, and metadata-rich chunking for knowledge-base extraction.
Built MinIO-synced ChromaDB vector store with Redis caching for production-ready retrieval infrastructure.

work.name: Vortigo (Part-Time)
highlights: Built internal assistant chatbots grounded on proprietary code and spreadsheet knowledge sources.
Delivered retrieval and prompt pipelines with production guardrails for repeatable workflows.

work.name: Condati
highlights: Led redesign of bid-optimization and forecasting ML systems for digital marketing workflows.
Built Python and SQL pipelines for production forecasting, anomaly detection, and decision support on Snowflake and Aurora-backed marketing data.
Partnered with business stakeholders to align model behavior with campaign KPIs and operational constraints.

Trained and tuned multi-level time-series models, including Prophet-based forecasting and regression-driven adjustments, with cross-validation, residual diagnostics, and quality gates using RMSE, MAE, MAPE, and MASE.
Implemented anomaly monitoring, alerting, and automated model-renewal logic for forecasting models in production.

work.name: ConstructIN
highlights: Led production computer-vision delivery for construction monitoring and analytics.
Coordinated model development, validation, and delivery workflows with client teams.
Led a team of 3 data scientists and delivered 4 production computer-vision applications for construction monitoring and progress analytics.
Developed segmentation-focused monitoring workflows and model-validation routines for site-progress measurement in real client deployments.

work.name: Samsung Research Brazil
highlights: Led wearable-health ML feature development deployed globally in Galaxy Watch products.
Delivered memory-efficient inference designs for constrained devices and real-time operation.
Coordinated cross-functional research-to-product delivery with HQ stakeholders.
Led end-to-end applied ML delivery from research and evaluation through global product rollout with HQ stakeholders.

work.name: Poatek IT Consulting
highlights: Delivered DS/ML projects across optimization, NLP, computer vision, and risk modeling.
Delivered applied ML work spanning optimization, NLP, computer vision, and risk modeling in client-facing consulting contexts.

education.institution: Federal University of Rio Grande do Sul
